---
title: "Setup & Environment Tests"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
---

## Overview

This page renders the live results of the last environment and setup validation test run.
The data is loaded from `test-results.json` at render time — re-render after running tests
to see updated results.

Run tests with:

```powershell
# Run all tests and save results (from repo root):
pytest tests/ -v --json-report --json-report-file=test-results.json

# Setup/environment checks only (requires Ollama running):
pytest tests/test_setup.py -v --json-report --json-report-file=test-results.json

# Ingestion pipeline tests only (no Ollama needed):
pytest tests/test_ingest.py -v --json-report --json-report-file=test-results.json
```

---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import json
import math
from pathlib import Path
from IPython.display import HTML, display

json_path = Path("../test-results.json")

if not json_path.exists():
    display(HTML(
        "<div style='padding:1em;background:#fff3cd;border:1px solid #ffc107;border-radius:4px'>"
        "<strong>No results file found.</strong> Run the pytest command above first.</div>"
    ))
else:
    data = json.loads(json_path.read_text())
    tests = data.get("tests", [])
    summary = data.get("summary", {})
    duration = data.get("duration", 0)
    exitcode = data.get("exitcode", -1)

    if not tests:
        display(HTML(
            "<div style='padding:1em;background:#fff3cd;border:1px solid #ffc107;border-radius:4px'>"
            f"<strong>No tests were collected</strong> (exit code {exitcode}). "
            "Check that <code>tests/test_setup.py</code> exists and pytest is run from the repo root."
            "</div>"
        ))
    else:
        # Build summary banner
        total   = summary.get("total", len(tests))
        passed  = summary.get("passed", 0)
        failed  = summary.get("failed", 0)
        skipped = summary.get("skipped", 0)
        colour  = "#d4edda" if failed == 0 else "#f8d7da"
        border  = "#28a745" if failed == 0 else "#dc3545"

        banner = (
            f"<div style='padding:1em;background:{colour};border-left:4px solid {border};"
            f"border-radius:4px;margin-bottom:1em'>"
            f"<strong>{'All tests passed' if failed == 0 else f'{failed} test(s) failed'}</strong>"
            f" &nbsp;|&nbsp; {passed} passed &nbsp; {failed} failed &nbsp; {skipped} skipped"
            f" &nbsp;|&nbsp; {duration:.2f}s"
            f"</div>"
        )

        # Build results table
        ICONS = {"passed": "✅", "failed": "❌", "skipped": "⚠️", "error": "💥"}

        rows = []
        for t in tests:
            node     = t.get("nodeid", "")
            outcome  = t.get("outcome", "unknown")
            dur      = t.get("duration") or t.get("call", {}).get("duration", 0) or 0
            icon     = ICONS.get(outcome, "❓")

            # Extract class and test name from nodeid
            parts = node.split("::")
            class_name = parts[1] if len(parts) > 1 else ""
            test_name  = parts[2] if len(parts) > 2 else parts[-1]
            friendly   = test_name.replace("test_", "").replace("_", " ").capitalize()

            # Error message for failures
            longrepr = ""
            if outcome == "failed":
                call = t.get("call", {})
                longrepr = call.get("longrepr", "")
                if longrepr:
                    longrepr = f"<br><code style='font-size:0.8em;color:#dc3545'>{longrepr[:300].replace(chr(10), '<br>')}</code>"

            rows.append(
                f"<tr>"
                f"<td>{icon} {outcome.upper()}</td>"
                f"<td>{class_name}</td>"
                f"<td>{friendly}{longrepr}</td>"
                f"<td style='text-align:right'>{dur:.3f}s</td>"
                f"</tr>"
            )

        table = (
            "<table style='width:100%;border-collapse:collapse'>"
            "<thead><tr style='border-bottom:2px solid #dee2e6'>"
            "<th style='text-align:left;padding:6px'>Status</th>"
            "<th style='text-align:left;padding:6px'>Class</th>"
            "<th style='text-align:left;padding:6px'>Test</th>"
            "<th style='text-align:right;padding:6px'>Duration</th>"
            "</tr></thead><tbody>"
            + "".join(rows)
            + "</tbody></table>"
        )

        display(HTML(banner + table))
```

---

## Test categories

### `tests/test_setup.py` — environment checks (local only, requires Ollama)

| Class | What it checks |
|-------|----------------|
| `TestDependencies` | LangChain, ChromaDB, RAG module importable |
| `TestOllamaService` | Ollama running on `localhost:11434` |
| `TestOllamaModels` | Default model (`OLLAMA_MODEL` env var) downloaded |
| `TestLLMProviders` | Ollama required; OpenAI / Anthropic / Gemini optional |
| `TestEnvironment` | Python ≥ 3.11, `.env` exists, required directories present |

### `tests/test_ingest.py` — ingestion pipeline (runs in CI, mocked dependencies)

| Test | What it checks |
|------|----------------|
| `test_load_speeches_csv` | CSV loads and converts to LangChain Documents with correct metadata |
| `test_chunk_documents` | Chunks preserve metadata and produce more chunks than source documents |
| `test_ingest_to_chromadb` | End-to-end ingestion with mocked Ollama and ChromaDB |
| `test_ingest_to_chromadb_missing_file` | Raises `FileNotFoundError` on missing CSV |
| `test_ingest_to_chromadb_empty_csv` | Raises `ValueError` on empty CSV |
| `test_real_csv_loads` | *(local only)* Real ECB CSV loads >100 speeches |
| `test_real_csv_metadata_shape` | *(local only)* First 10 real speeches have required metadata keys |
| `test_real_csv_chunking` | *(local only)* First 5 real speeches chunk correctly |

## Troubleshooting

**Ollama not running**

```powershell
ollama serve   # start in a separate terminal
```

**Model missing**

```powershell
ollama list                  # see installed models
ollama pull llama3.2:1b      # download the default
```

**Import errors** — re-sync dependencies:

```powershell
uv sync
```

## Next steps

Once all tests pass, proceed to **Phase 1: Document Ingestion**.
See the [Project Report](report.qmd) for details.
